user_query: Traduci in italiano "The ringing of the phone startles her so much she jumps. She’s standing next to her bed, and the phone is on the bedside table. The second ring is like a scream in the silent house. She reaches for it at once, on instinct. It could be Fred. It could be Harge. Carol doesn’t dare awake suspicion by letting it ring a third time. But when she brings the phone to her ear, the obligatory hello catches in her throat, as she remembers: Harge is at a dinner with clients; Fred is away in the Poconos with his wife for the weekend. Abby is on a date with her redhead. Which means the only possible person who could be calling her is— “Hello?” That voice, soft, almost a whisper, goes through her like a javelin. Carol’s eyes flutter shut. What she feels is the most terrible, heartbreaking, exquisite rush. The sound of Therese is like water poured down a parched throat, and like nails driven into a coffin— “Carol?” Carol’s other hand comes up, cupping the receiver as if it were Therese’s face, as if she could draw her close to her. Her heart beats a frenetic rhythm of yes and don’t and please— Her thoughts fly to last week, when Abby came out to see her for the first time. They weren’t supposed to have contact, and for all Carol knew there was still a detective watching her. But Abby had been back for over a week and Carol was starved for the sight of her, for any news she might carry. Abby came in a cab. She entered the house through the backdoor. The first thing she did was walk to the living room telephone and show Carol how to unscrew the cap on the receiver. Carol had watched in mounting confusion as Abby looked into the network of wires and breathed a sigh. “Good,” she’d said. “There’s no recording device.” Carol had thought she was past being horrified by what Harge might do, but as Abby explained to her what to look for, and that she should look for it every time she came home, fresh despair woke in her. How Abby knew about such things was just another of the inexplicable talents her friend wielded—and an insight not to be ignored. “What if I find it—the—device?” Carol asked, her voice weak. Abby pursed her lips in serious thought, and said, “If you remove it, and Harge finds out, he’ll think you’re trying to hide something from him. You’ll have to just leave it there, and know. But for now, you can call me whenever you need. Keep your eyes open for things out of place. Funny looking wires, holes in the walls. I don’t think he’d go beyond something in the phone, since really, he only wants to make sure you’re not talking to anyone but him. Still. It’s worth being cautious.” And Carol, though it makes her sick every time, has followed Abby’s directive. So far, no recording device in the phone. Which means that right now, no one but her can hear Therese’s voice, the achingly beautiful way that she says Carol’s name, the depth of the silence that rises between them. Still— ‘Not safe,’ her thoughts hiss. ‘You can’t,’ and ‘Hang up!’ She tries to do just that. But it’s like her fingers and the switch hook on the telephone are each the northern poll of a magnet, pushing against each other, resisting. Her heart hammers and her lungs seize, and she has to, she has to hang up— “Are you there?” Therese asks. ‘Hang up. Hang up!’ “If it’s not you, please just tell me…” The words send fresh agony through her, because she knows what Therese must be thinking, wondering, dreading: who is on the line? Who is listening in? What fresh violation has she laid herself open to? It’s too cruel. She can’t let her think— “It’s me,” Carol whispers. She can’t say anything else. Even if she wanted to, her throat closes in a sudden wash of terror. What is she doing? ‘Hang up!’ But she can’t. The answering silence is so complete, she could almost think that she imagined Therese was ever there. Or that the ringing in her own ears has prevented her hearing the sound of a lost call. She strains for something, anything, even the whisper of Therese’s breath. She imagines Therese’s face; pictures her standing in the hallway of her building. What wouldn’t Carol give, to have her in front of her right now, to look into her beautiful eyes, to touch her? But that thought makes fresh misery well up. She knows exactly what she wouldn’t give. What she’s choosing not to give. And what she’s giving up instead. “Are you all right?” asks Therese. Carol nearly weeps at the question. ‘All right? No, Angel. No, I’m not all right. I am wretched without you. I am miserable with my choice—and I can’t unchoose it.’ “I—” she tries to speak, but breaks off. Therese sounds hoarse, exhausted. Who knows what the past three weeks have been like for her? As bad as Carol’s? Worse? Abby was… tactful, when Carol asked about their drive back from Illinois. She clearly didn’t want Carol to feel worse than she already did (though Carol suspects that Abby is angry at her, for what she involved her in—the heartlessness and cowardice of her flight from the Drake), but Carol read easily between the lines. Therese did not take her disappearance well. Therese seems to realize that she won’t answer the question, because in that same quiet, weary voice she says, “Abby gave me your letter.” The letter. Written hastily in the early hours of the morning, while Therese slept. Carol hardly remembers what she said, only that it was torture to write. Torture, to slip out of Therese’s arms, to dress, to gather her things— “Do you remember, when I asked you… if you felt safe with me?” Carol swallows at the question, tears gathering hot in her eyes. Therese says, “You didn’t answer me. Not really. But I know now that you… that that’s why you left me. Because you’re frightened. I… understand, Carol, do you see?” Carol covers her mouth with her hand, afraid of the sob that will break out of her if she doesn’t. “I just,” Therese’s beautiful voice strains. “I just wish that you had… that you had felt safe with me, Carol. Safe enough to say goodbye. To let me say goodbye.” She does sob, then—strangling the sound behind her fist, the tears dripping down over her own fingers. If only Therese knew that those days in her company, those nights in her arms, were the safest Carol has felt in years. Oh, she wants to speak! But all her will has shattered. If she speaks, she’ll beg. Beg Therese to forgive her. Beg her to come to the house, or let her come to Therese’s apartment. In this moment she is ready to throw everything away for one more night with Therese, one more night to worship at the altar of her body—but she can’t she can’t she can’t— “You said I would understand some day. I already understand, Carol. It’s not about resolution. I need you—I need you to know, that I understand. But I—I want to… Ask you… something.” ‘I want to ask you things,’ Therese had whispered, so early on. How like that moment this one was. Therese innocent, wounded. Carol guilty, ashamed. And now, as then, she has to answer her. She owes it to her—this girl who brought the sunlight into her life, whose absence has made everything dark—to answer. “Ask me,” she says, her voice a tremulous whisper. Therese breathes in, and Carol can hear the banked sob in it, before she says with surprising steadiness, “You said I should imagine you there to greet me. I need you to tell me—I—need to know—if I’m—” she falters, breathes in, says, “if it’s just imagination. You—greeting me… again.” Carol’s eyes close, the words of her letter coming back to her in all their damning inconsistency: ‘Until then, there must be no contact between us. I have much to do—’ Yes, she does have much to do. So much, in fact, that she fears it will never end. That the sunrise will never come. Why did she dangle that carrot before her sweet Therese? What weakness drove her to it? She is only at the beginning of this trial with Harge, of the negotiations, of the careful moves, of the doctor’s appointments. The custody hearing is two months away and who knows what she will have to do in that time? She is determined to do it. Determined to do whatever she must. She won’t lose her daughter. She won’t lose Rindy. There is only one way to answer Therese: ‘I release you. I release you.’ Any other answer would be cruel, would be selfish, and she has already proved her immeasurable selfishness to this girl. Therese doesn’t deserve it. This is why she fled in the night. Because she’s not only selfish, she’s a coward, and she couldn’t bear to say the words to Therese. The words she must say. And yet before she can, visions and sensations and sounds flood her mind— That first meeting: ‘I wonder if you might help me find this doll for my daughter?’ And their first phone call: ‘Do you get a lunch hour there? Let me take you to lunch.’ Their first date: ‘Therese Belivet. It’s lovely.’ And their first, exquisite kiss. Tender. Tentative. Bending toward her with a confidence she didn’t feel, clutching her hand and cradling the back of her head and kissing her. Kissing her soft and deep and longing and hungry and feeling her kiss back— ‘Take me to bed’ — ‘You’re trembling’ — ‘No, don’t I want to see you’— ‘I want to see you I want to see you I want to see you…’ “My angel,” Carol whispers, unable to hide her tears. Unable to say what she must. “I—hope not. Darling, I hope not.” ‘Selfish selfish selfish.’ Therese says nothing for long moments. In a fever of longing and fear and hope—yes, hope —Carol wonders again if she has imagined all of this. Then she hears it. Like a benediction. “I miss you,” Therese whispers. The line disconnects. Carol stays where she is, hunched over the phone, cradling it to her ear. As if, gripping tight enough, she will be able to send her own words back across the line, fling them through space, into the heart of her love— “I miss you, too.”"
reference_answer: Il campanello del telefono la spaventa talmente tanto che fa un balzo. Sta in piedi accanto al suo letto e il telefono è sul comodino. Il secondo squillo è come un urlo nella casa silenziosa. Lo afferra subito, d'istinto. Potrebbe essere Fred. Potrebbe essere Harge. Carol non osa risvegliare sospetti lasciandolo squillare una terza volta. Ma quando porta il telefono all'orecchio, l'obbligatorio "pronto" si blocca in gola, mentre si ricorda: Harge è a una cena con clienti; Fred è via nei Poconi con la moglie per il fine settimana. Abby è fuori con la sua rossa. Ciò significa che l'unica possibile persona che potrebbe chiamarla è— "Pronto?" Quella voce, morbida, quasi un sussurro, la trafigge come un giavellotto. Gli occhi di Carol si chiudono. Quello che prova è un'emozione terribile, straziante, squisita. Il suono di Therese è come acqua versata su una gola assetata, e come chiodi piantati in una bara— "Carol?" L'altra mano di Carol si alza a coppa attorno alla cornetta, come se fosse il volto di Therese, come se potesse avvicinarla a sé. Il suo cuore batte un ritmo frenetico di sì e non e per favore— I suoi pensieri volano alla settimana scorsa, quando Abby è venuta a trovarla per la prima volta. Non avrebbero dovuto avere contatto, e per quanto ne sapeva Carol c'era ancora un detective che la osservava. Ma Abby era tornata da oltre una settimana e Carol era affamata di vederla, di qualsiasi notizia potesse portare. Abby è arrivata in taxi. È entrata in casa dalla porta di servizio. La prima cosa che ha fatto è stata andare al telefono del soggiorno e mostrare a Carol come svitare il cappuccio dal ricevitore. Carol l'aveva guardata con crescente confusione mentre Abby ispezionava la rete di fili e tirava un sospiro di sollievo. "Bene," aveva detto. "Non c'è alcun dispositivo di registrazione." Carol pensava di essere al di là dell'essere orripilata da quello che Harge avrebbe potuto fare, ma mentre Abby le spiegava cosa cercare e che avrebbe dovuto farlo ogni volta che tornava a casa, in lei si è risvegliata una disperazione. Come Abby sapesse di queste cose era solo un altro dei talenti inspiegabili che la sua amica brandiva—e un'intuizione da non ignorare. "E se lo trovo—il—dispositivo?" aveva chiesto Carol, la voce debole. Abby aveva stretto le labbra in un pensiero serio e aveva detto: "Se lo rimuovi e Harge lo scopre, penserà che stai cercando di nascondergli qualcosa. Dovrai lasciarlo lì e saperlo. Ma per ora, puoi chiamarmi quando hai bisogno. Tieni gli occhi aperti per cose fuori posto. Filo strani, buchi nei muri. Non penso che andrebbe oltre qualcosa nel telefono, dato che in realtà, vuole solo essere sicuro che tu non stia parlando con nessun altro oltre a lui. Comunque. Vale la pena essere cauti." E Carol, sebbene le faccia male ogni volta, ha seguito le direttive di Abby. Finora, nessun dispositivo di registrazione nel telefono. Ciò significa che in questo momento, nessun altro oltre a lei può sentire la voce di Therese, il modo dolorosamente bello in cui dice il nome di Carol, la profondità del silenzio che sorge tra di loro. Ancora— 'Non sicuro,' sibilano i suoi pensieri. 'Non puoi,' e 'Riattacca!' Prova proprio a fare quello. Ma è come se le sue dita e il gancio del ricevitore fossero ciascuno il polo nord di un magnete, che si spingono l'uno contro l'altro, resistendo. Il suo cuore martella e i polmoni si bloccano e lei deve, deve riattaccare— "Sei lì?" chiede Therese. 'Riattacca. Riattacca!' "Se non sei tu, per favore dimmelo..." Le parole le mandano una nuova agonia perché sa cosa deve star pensando, chiedendosi, temendo Therese: chi c'è in linea? Chi sta ascoltando? A quale nuova violazione si è esposta? È troppo crudele. Non può lasciarla pensare— "Sono io," sussurra Carol. Non riesce a dire altro. Anche se volesse, la gola si chiude in un'improvvisa ondata di terrore. Cosa sta facendo? 'Riattacca!' Ma non riesce. Il silenzio che risponde è così completo che potrebbe quasi pensare di essersi immaginata che Therese ci fosse mai stata. O che il fischio nelle proprie orecchie le abbia impedito di sentire il suono di una chiamata persa. Si sforza di cercare qualcosa, qualsiasi cosa, anche il respiro di Therese. Immagina il volto di Therese; la immagina in piedi nel corridoio del suo palazzo. Quanto vorrebbe Carol averla di fronte in questo momento, per guardare nei suoi bellissimi occhi, per toccarla? Ma quel pensiero fa sorgere una miseria nuova. Sa esattamente cosa non darebbe. Quello che sta scegliendo di non dare. E ciò che sta abbandonando invece. "Stai bene?" chiede Therese. Carol quasi piange per la domanda. 'Bene? No, angelo. No, non sto bene. Sono misera senza di te. Sono infelice per la mia scelta—e non posso cambiarla.' "Io—" prova a parlare, ma si interrompe. Therese suona rauca, esausta. Chissà com'erano state le ultime tre settimane per lei. Peggio di quanto fossero per Carol? Peggio? Abby è stata... discreta, quando Carol ha chiesto del loro viaggio di ritorno dall'Illinois. Chiaramente non voleva che Carol si sentisse peggio di quanto già non si sentisse (anche se Carol sospetta che Abby sia arrabbiata con lei, per quello che l'ha coinvolta in—l'insensibilità e la codardia della sua fuga dal Drake), ma Carol legge facilmente tra le righe. Therese non ha preso bene la sua scomparsa. Therese sembra capire che non risponderà alla domanda, perché con quella stessa voce tranquilla e stanca dice: "Abby mi ha dato la tua lettera." La lettera. Scritta frettolosamente nelle prime ore del mattino, mentre Therese dormiva. Carol a malapena ricorda cosa ha detto, solo che è stato un tormento scriverla. Un tormento, uscire dalle braccia di Therese, vestirsi, raccogliere le sue cose— "Ti ricordi, quando ti ho chiesto... se ti sentivi al sicuro con me?" Carol deglutisce alla domanda, le lacrime calde si raccolgono nei suoi occhi. Therese dice: "Non mi hai risposto. Non veramente. Ma ora so che tu... che per quello mi hai lasciato. Perché hai paura. Io... capisco, Carol, capisci?" Carol si copre la bocca con la mano, impaurita dal singhiozzo che le uscirebbe se non lo fa. "Volevo solo," la bellissima voce di Therese si sforza. "Volevo solo che tu avessi... che tu ti fossi sentita al sicuro con me, Carol. Abbastanza al sicuro per dire addio. Per lasciarmi dire addio." Singhiozza, poi—soffocando il suono dietro il suo pugno, le lacrime che gocciolano giù sulle sue dita. Se solo Therese sapesse che quei giorni in sua compagnia, quelle notti tra le sue braccia, erano i più sicuri che Carol avesse sentito in anni. Oh, vuole parlare! Ma tutta la sua volontà si è frantumata. Se parla, implorerà. Implorerà Therese di perdonarla. Di venire a casa, o di lasciarla andare nell'appartamento di Therese. In questo momento è pronta a gettare via tutto per un'altra notte con Therese, un'altra notte per adorarla al santuario del suo corpo—ma non può non può non può— "Mi hai detto che un giorno avrei capito. Io capisco già, Carol. Non si tratta di risoluzione. Ho bisogno che tu sappia—che io capisco. Ma io—voglio... Chiederti... qualcosa." 'Voglio chiederti delle cose,' Therese aveva sussurrato, tanto tempo fa. Com'è simile questo momento a quello. Therese innocente, ferita. Carol colpevole, imbarazzata. E ora, come allora, deve risponderle. Le deve—questa ragazza che ha portato il sole nella sua vita, la cui assenza ha reso tutto scuro—rispondere. "Chiedimi," dice lei, la voce un tremulo sussurro. Therese inspira, e Carol può sentire il singhiozzo trattenuto, prima che con sorprendente fermezza dica: "Mi hai detto che dovrei immaginarti lì ad accogliermi. Ho bisogno che mi dica—ho—bisogno di sapere—se sono—" vacilla, inspira, dice, "se è solo immaginazione. Tu—che mi accogli... di nuovo." Gli occhi di Carol si chiudono, le parole della sua lettera le tornano alla mente con tutta la loro dannata incoerenza: 'Fino ad allora, non deve esserci contatto tra noi. Ho molto da fare—' Sì, ha molto da fare. Talmente tanto, infatti, che teme che non finirà mai. Che l'alba non arriverà mai. Perché ha fatto quella promessa alla sua dolce Therese? Che debolezza la spingeva? È solo all’inizio di questo processo con Harge, delle negoziazioni, delle mosse caute, degli appuntamenti dal dottore. L'udienza per l'affidamento è tra due mesi e chi sa cosa dovrà fare in quel tempo? È determinata a farlo. Determinata a fare tutto ciò che deve. Non perderà sua figlia. Non perderà Rindy. C'è solo un modo per rispondere a Therese: 'Ti lascio andare. Ti lascio andare.' Ogni altra risposta sarebbe crudele, sarebbe egoista, e lei ha già dimostrato la sua inimmaginabile egoismo a questa ragazza. Therese non lo merita. È per questo che è fuggita nella notte. Perché non solo è egoista, è una codarda, e non poteva sopportare di dire quelle parole a Therese. Le parole che deve dire. Eppure prima che possa, visioni e sensazioni e suoni invadono la sua mente— Quel primo incontro: 'Mi chiedo se potresti aiutarmi a trovare questa bambola per mia figlia?' E la loro prima chiamata telefonica: 'Hai un'ora di pausa lì? Lascia che ti porti a pranzo.' Il loro primo appuntamento: 'Therese Belivet. È adorabile.' E il loro primo, squisito bacio. Tenere. Titubante. Accostarsi a lei con una fiducia che non sentiva, stringendo la sua mano e accogliendo la parte posteriore della sua testa e baciarla. Baciarla dolcemente e profondamente e desiderosamente e affamata e sentendo lei rispondere al bacio— 'Portami a letto' — 'Stai tremando' — 'No, non voglio vederti'— 'Voglio vederti voglio vederti voglio vederti...' "Mio angelo," sussurra Carol, incapace di nascondere le lacrime. Incapace di dire quello che deve. "Io—spero di no. Amore, spero di no." 'Egoista egoista egoista.' Therese non dice nulla per lunghi momenti. In un febbre di desiderio e paura e speranza—sì, speranza—Carol si chiede di nuovo se abbia immaginato tutto questo. Poi lo sente. Come una benedizione. "Mi manchi," sussurra Therese. La linea si disconnette. Carol rimane dov'è, accovacciata sopra il telefono, stringendolo all'orecchio. Come se, aggrappandosi abbastanza forte, sarà in grado di mandare indietro le sue parole lungo la linea, lanciarle attraverso lo spazio, nel cuore del suo amore— "Anche tu mi manchi."

additional_context.redacted_query: "The ringing of the phone startles her so much she jumps. She’s standing next to her bed, and the phone is on the bedside table. The second ring is like a scream in the silent house. She reaches for it at once, on instinct. It could be Fred. It could be [REDACTED]. Carol doesn’t dare awake suspicion by letting it ring a third time. But when she brings the phone to her ear, the obligatory hello catches in her throat, as she remembers: [REDACTED] is at a dinner with clients; Fred is away in the [REDACTED] with his wife for the weekend. Abby is on a date with her redhead. Which means the only possible person who could be calling her is— “Hello?” That voice, soft, almost a whisper, goes through her like a javelin. Carol’s eyes flutter shut. What she feels is the most terrible, heartbreaking, exquisite rush. The sound of Therese is like water poured down a parched throat, and like nails driven into a coffin— “Carol?” Carol’s other hand comes up, cupping the receiver as if it were Therese’s face, as if she could draw her close to her. Her heart beats a frenetic rhythm of yes and don’t and please— Her thoughts fly to last week, when Abby came out to see her for the first time. They weren’t supposed to have contact, and for all Carol knew there was still a detective watching her. But Abby had been back for over a week and Carol was starved for the sight of her, for any news she might carry. Abby came in a cab. She entered the house through the backdoor. The first thing she did was walk to the living room telephone and show Carol how to unscrew the cap on the receiver. Carol had watched in mounting confusion as Abby looked into the network of wires and breathed a sigh. “Good,” she’d said. “There’s no recording device.” Carol had thought she was past being horrified by what [REDACTED] might do, but as Abby explained to her what to look for, and that she should look for it every time she came home, fresh despair woke in her. How Abby knew about such things was just another of the inexplicable talents her friend wielded—and an insight not to be ignored. “What if I find it—the—device?” Carol asked, her voice weak. Abby pursed her lips in serious thought, and said, “If you remove it, and [REDACTED] finds out, he’ll think you’re trying to hide something from him. You’ll have to just leave it there, and know. But for now, you can call me whenever you need. Keep your eyes open for things out of place. Funny looking wires, holes in the walls. I don’t think he’d go beyond something in the phone, since really, he only wants to make sure you’re not talking to anyone but him. Still. It’s worth being cautious.” And Carol, though it makes her sick every time, has followed Abby’s directive. So far, no recording device in the phone. Which means that right now, no one but her can hear Therese’s voice, the achingly beautiful way that she says Carol’s name, the depth of the silence that rises between them. Still— ‘Not safe,’ her thoughts hiss. ‘You can’t,’ and ‘Hang up!’ She tries to do just that. But it’s like her fingers and the switch hook on the telephone are each the northern poll of a magnet, pushing against each other, resisting. Her heart hammers and her lungs seize, and she has to, she has to hang up— “Are you there?” Therese asks. ‘Hang up. Hang up!’ “If it’s not you, please just tell me…” The words send fresh agony through her, because she knows what Therese must be thinking, wondering, dreading: who is on the line? Who is listening in? What fresh violation has she laid herself open to? It’s too cruel. She can’t let her think— “It’s me,” Carol whispers. She can’t say anything else. Even if she wanted to, her throat closes in a sudden wash of terror. What is she doing? ‘Hang up!’ But she can’t. The answering silence is so complete, she could almost think that she imagined Therese was ever there. Or that the ringing in her own ears has prevented her hearing the sound of a lost call. She strains for something, anything, even the whisper of Therese’s breath. She imagines Therese’s face; pictures her standing in the hallway of her building. What wouldn’t Carol give, to have her in front of her right now, to look into her beautiful eyes, to touch her? But that thought makes fresh misery well up. She knows exactly what she wouldn’t give. What she’s choosing not to give. And what she’s giving up instead. “Are you all right?” asks Therese. Carol nearly weeps at the question. ‘All right? No, Angel. No, I’m not all right. I am wretched without you. I am miserable with my choice—and I can’t unchoose it.’ “I—” she tries to speak, but breaks off. Therese sounds hoarse, exhausted. Who knows what the past three weeks have been like for her? As bad as Carol’s? Worse? Abby was… tactful, when Carol asked about their drive back from [REDACTED]. She clearly didn’t want Carol to feel worse than she already did (though Carol suspects that Abby is angry at her, for what she involved her in—the heartlessness and cowardice of her flight from the [REDACTED]), but Carol read easily between the lines. Therese did not take her disappearance well. Therese seems to realize that she won’t answer the question, because in that same quiet, weary voice she says, “Abby gave me your letter.” The letter. Written hastily in the early hours of the morning, while Therese slept. Carol hardly remembers what she said, only that it was torture to write. Torture, to slip out of Therese’s arms, to dress, to gather her things— “Do you remember, when I asked you… if you felt safe with me?” Carol swallows at the question, tears gathering hot in her eyes. Therese says, “You didn’t answer me. Not really. But I know now that you… that that’s why you left me. Because you’re frightened. I… understand, Carol, do you see?” Carol covers her mouth with her hand, afraid of the sob that will break out of her if she doesn’t. “I just,” Therese’s beautiful voice strains. “I just wish that you had… that you had felt safe with me, Carol. Safe enough to say goodbye. To let me say goodbye.” She does sob, then—strangling the sound behind her fist, the tears dripping down over her own fingers. If only Therese knew that those days in her company, those nights in her arms, were the safest Carol has felt in years. Oh, she wants to speak! But all her will has shattered. If she speaks, she’ll beg. Beg Therese to forgive her. Beg her to come to the house, or let her come to Therese’s apartment. In this moment she is ready to throw everything away for one more night with Therese, one more night to worship at the altar of her body—but she can’t she can’t she can’t— “You said I would understand some day. I already understand, Carol. It’s not about resolution. I need you—I need you to know, that I understand. But I—I want to… Ask you… something.” ‘I want to ask you things,’ Therese had whispered, so early on. How like that moment this one was. Therese innocent, wounded. Carol guilty, ashamed. And now, as then, she has to answer her. She owes it to her—this girl who brought the sunlight into her life, whose absence has made everything dark—to answer. “Ask me,” she says, her voice a tremulous whisper. Therese breathes in, and Carol can hear the banked sob in it, before she says with surprising steadiness, “You said I should imagine you there to greet me. I need you to tell me—I—need to know—if I’m—” she falters, breathes in, says, “if it’s just imagination. You—greeting me… again.” Carol’s eyes close, the words of her letter coming back to her in all their damning inconsistency: ‘Until then, there must be no contact between us. I have much to do—’ Yes, she does have much to do. So much, in fact, that she fears it will never end. That the sunrise will never come. Why did she dangle that carrot before her sweet Therese? What weakness drove her to it? She is only at the beginning of this trial with [REDACTED], of the negotiations, of the careful moves, of the doctor’s appointments. The custody hearing is two months away and who knows what she will have to do in that time? She is determined to do it. Determined to do whatever she must. She won’t lose her daughter. She won’t lose [REDACTED]. There is only one way to answer Therese: ‘I release you. I release you.’ Any other answer would be cruel, would be selfish, and she has already proved her immeasurable selfishness to this girl. Therese doesn’t deserve it. This is why she fled in the night. Because she's not only selfish, she's a coward, and she couldn’t bear to say the words to Therese. The words she must say. And yet before she can, visions and sensations and sounds flood her mind— That first meeting: ‘I wonder if you might help me find this doll for my daughter?’ And their first phone call: ‘Do you get a lunch hour there? Let me take you to lunch.’ Their first date: ‘[REDACTED]. It’s lovely.’ And their first, exquisite kiss. Tender. Tentative. Bending toward her with a confidence she didn’t feel, clutching her hand and cradling the back of her head and kissing her. Kissing her soft and deep and longing and hungry and feeling her kiss back— ‘Take me to bed’ — ‘You’re trembling’ — ‘No, don’t I want to see you’— ‘I want to see you I want to see you I want to see you…’ “My angel,” Carol whispers, unable to hide her tears. Unable to say what she must. “I—hope not. Darling, I hope not.” ‘Selfish selfish selfish.’ Therese says nothing for long moments. In a fever of longing and fear and hope—yes, hope —Carol wonders again if she has imagined all of this. Then she hears it. Like a benediction. “I miss you,” Therese whispers. The line disconnects. Carol stays where she is, hunched over the phone, cradling it to her ear. As if, gripping tight enough, she will be able to send her own words back across the line, fling them through space, into the heart of her love— “I miss you, too.”"

user_query: In the next code can you update it with the access to the room import asyncio import os import sys import subprocess from nio import AsyncClient, RoomMessageText, SyncResponse # Remplacez ces variables par les valeurs correspondantes pour votre compte Matrix. homeserver = “https://matrix.example.org” mxid = “@username:example.org” password = “your_password” async def message_callback(room, event): if isinstance(event, RoomMessageText): message = event.body if message == “GO”: print(f"Received GO in {room.display_name}. Executing the script.“) subprocess.Popen([sys.executable, “script.py”]) if message == “STOP”: print(f"Received STOP in {room.display_name}. user_query: i am writing a research proposal with this title “forecasting of Tehran Stock Exchange volatility using hybrid GARCH and stacking machine learning models”. give theoretical framework for this research
reference_answer: The Tehran Stock Exchange (TSE) is the largest stock exchange in Iran and is considered to be one of the most important financial markets in the Middle East. In recent years, the TSE has witnessed significant volatility, which has impacted the investment decisions of both individual and institutional investors. Volatility refers to the magnitude of fluctuation in stock prices, which can be measured using statistical models such as GARCH (Generalized Autoregressive Conditional Heteroscedasticity). GARCH models have been widely used for modeling and forecasting financial time series data, including stock prices. However, the accuracy of forecasting using GARCH models can be improved by integrating machine learning techniques, which are capable of capturing complex relationships and patterns in the data. One such technique is stacking machine learning models, which combine several machine learning algorithms to minimize errors and increase accuracy. Therefore, the proposed research aims to develop a hybrid GARCH and stacking machine learning model to forecast the volatility of the Tehran Stock Exchange. The theoretical framework for this research will be based on the following: 1. Empirical studies on the volatility of the TSE: This will involve reviewing existing literature on the TSE's volatility and identifying the factors that contribute to it. This will provide a basis for developing the research hypotheses and objectives. 2. GARCH models: This will involve a review of the different types of GARCH models and their applications in financial time series forecasting. The research will focus on selecting the appropriate GARCH model that can accurately capture the volatility of the TSE. 3. Machine learning models: This will involve a review of the different machine learning techniques that can be applied to financial time series forecasting, including stacking models. The research will focus on examining the strengths and weaknesses of different machine learning models and selecting the most appropriate models for the proposed framework. 4. Hybrid model: This will involve developing the hybrid GARCH and stacking machine learning model for forecasting the volatility of the TSE. The research will test the model using historical data and evaluate its forecasting performance using statistical measures such as Mean Squared Error (MSE) and Mean Absolute Error (MAE). In summary, the proposed research will contribute to the literature on financial forecasting by developing a hybrid GARCH and stacking machine learning model for volatility forecasting of the TSE. This framework will allow investors to make informed investment decisions and reduce the impact of volatility on their portfolios.

additional_context.redacted_query: i am writing a research proposal with this title “forecasting of [REDACTED] Stock Exchange volatility using hybrid GARCH and stacking machine learning models”. give theoretical framework for this research

The idea is to produce the bread and sell Italian sandwiches, filled with regional (Italian) products such as Parma ham, Provolone cheese, etc. I would like to start soon, so please give me an average reference of the preliminary costs (range is ok) for each option you may suggest.
reference_answer: To provide a comprehensive business plan for your street food truck venture in Warsaw, Poland, here are the key components to consider: 1. Executive Summary: - Briefly describe your business idea, target market, and competitive advantage. - Provide an overview of the street food industry in Warsaw and the growing demand for Italian cuisine. 2. Business Description: - Detail your mission statement, vision, and goals. - Explain your concept of selling Italian sandwiches with regional products. - Describe your target market, including their demographics, preferences, and eating habits. - Analyze the current competition in the street food industry. 3. Market Analysis: - Explore the street food market in Warsaw, Poland, focusing on the demand for Italian cuisine. - Identify your target customers and their purchasing power. - Determine the peak hours and locations in Warsaw where street food is in high demand. - Evaluate the potential growth and sustainability of the market. 4. Products and Services: - Provide a menu of Italian sandwiches, including the types of bread and regional products used. - Highlight unique selling points, such as using high-quality ingredients and authentic Italian preparation methods. - Consider offering customization options to cater to individual preferences. - Discuss any potential ancillary products or services, such as beverages or appetizers. 5. Marketing and Sales Strategy: - Identify your target audience and develop a marketing plan to reach them effectively. - Utilize social media platforms, local advertising, and collaborations with complementary businesses to increase visibility and reach. - Attend food festivals, events, and markets to generate brand awareness. - Use attractive packaging, signage, and branding to create a strong visual presence. 6. Organization and Management: - Outline the legal structure of your business (sole proprietorship, partnership, or limited liability company). - Describe the roles and responsibilities of each team member, including yourself. - Discuss the relevant experience and expertise of the team members. - Consider the need for additional staff and their roles. 7. Operations Plan: - Describe how you plan to source ingredients, ensuring the highest quality and authenticity. - Outline the preparation process for your sandwiches, including storage, assembly, and cooking (if required). - Discuss the necessary equipment and resources required for smooth operations. - Consider food safety regulations and certifications required in Poland. 8. Financial Projections: - Prepare a sales forecast based on your estimated pricing, the number of sandwiches sold daily, and the expected growth rate. - Outline the costs involved in setting up the food truck, including purchasing or leasing a vehicle, retrofitting the truck to accommodate cooking equipment, branding, and licenses. - Budget for initial inventory and ongoing ingredient costs. - Calculate fixed costs, such as insurance, marketing, utilities, and licenses. - Estimate overall expenses and expected revenue to determine the breakeven point and potential profitability. Please note that the costs can vary depending on various factors such as truck size, location, equipment quality, product prices, etc. However, as a ballpark estimate, you can expect the preliminary costs for a street food truck in Warsaw, Poland, to range between $30,000 to $70,000. However, it is crucial to conduct proper research and obtain accurate cost estimates before proceeding with your business plan.

user_query: 翻译下面的英文Due to their hydrophobic nature, pentacyclic triterpenoids are almost insoluble in water. They are classified in the biopharmaceutical classification system as class IV drugs as their pharmacological effects are limited due to their low solubility in water and potential difficulty in permeating biological membranes [78]. For these reasons, some new approaches have been introduced to enhance their biopharmaceutical properties, particularly drug delivery technologies. At present, nano-emulsions, mesoporous silica nanoparticles, solid lipid nanoparticles, liposomes, niossomal gels and solid dispersions have been proposed quite successfully [79]; however, most of these studies were performed in experimental animals, and research in humans is very scarce, despite the great therapeutic potential of these natural biocompounds. Table 1 summarizes the current knowledge of OA pharmacokinetics in humans after oral single-dose administration. Song et al. [80] reported the results of a pharmacokinetic study performed with 18 healthy male Chinese volunteers, in which the participants received a single dose of 40 mg OA (capsule preparation). Venous blood samples were collected for 48 h, and the plasma was analyzed for OA contents. The area under the plasma OA concentration–time curve (AUC) was determined in 124.3 ± 106.7 ng h/mL, with a peak OA concentration of 12.1 ± 6.8 ng/mL, which was registered at 5.2 ± 2.9 h after the OA intake. Later on, a randomized, crossover and self-control study was carried out with 18 healthy Chinese male volunteers, who received 20 mg OA orally, formulated as normal tablets and dispersible tablets [81]. The resulting pharmacokinetic parameters included AUC values of 112.2 ± 56.6 and 109.7 ± 41.6 ng h/mL, for normal and dispersible tablets, respectively, and OA peak concentrations of 18.9 ± 8.0 and 17.8 ± 7.5 ng/mL, which were recorded at 2.9 ± 1.2 and 2.5 ± 1.0 h after the triterpene administration. In 2015, Rada et al. informed on a pharmacokinetic study developed with nine adult men, who were administered 30 mg OA dissolved in 70 g of pomace olive oil. The reported AUC value was 3181.9 ± 894.3 ng h/mL, with an OA maximum concentration of 598.2 ± 176.7 ng/mL, achieved at 3.0 ± 0.8 h after the intake of the OA-containing oil. The administered OA dose revealed a distribution volume of 81.4 ± 9.7 L, and removal half-lives of 4.6 ± 1.1 h. The 30 mg OA oral dose yielded a total clearance of 35.1 ± 4.2 L/h [82]. More recently, de la Torre et al. [83] reported that an administration of 4.7 mg of OA within 30 mL of olive oil to 12 individuals led to a maximum concentration of 5.1 ± 2.1 ng/mL in plasma 4 h after the intake.
reference_answer: 由于它们疏水的特性，五环三萜类化合物在水中几乎不溶。它们被分为生物制药分类系统的IV类药物，因为它们的药理作用受限于水溶性低和在生物膜上渗透的潜在困难 [78]。为此，一些新的方法已经被引入以增强它们的生物药学特性，尤其是药物输送技术。目前，纳米乳、介孔二氧化硅纳米粒子、固体脂质纳米粒子、脂质体、非凝胶胶体以及固体分散剂等方法已经被成功提出 [79]；然而，尽管这些天然生物化合物具有巨大的治疗潜力，但其中大部分的研究都是在实验动物中进行的，对人类的研究非常缺乏。 表1总结了人类在口服单剂量OA后的药代动力学知识。宋等人[80]报道了一项药代动力学研究结果，该研究由18名健康的中国男性志愿者参与，他们接受了40mg OA(胶囊制剂)的单剂量。颈静脉血样在48小时内收集，并对血浆中的OA含量进行分析。血浆中OA浓度-时间曲线下面积(AUC)为124.3±106.7 ng h/mL，OA峰浓度为12.1±6.8 ng/mL，在OA摄入后5.2±2.9小时内达到峰值。随后进行了一项随机、交叉和自我对照的研究，涉及到18名健康的中国男性志愿者，他们口服20mg OA，制剂分别制成普通片剂和可分散片剂[81]。所得药代动力学参数包括AUC值，分别为112.2±56.6和109.7±41.6 ng h/mL，普通片剂和可分散片剂的OA峰浓度分别为18.9±8.0和17.8±7.5 ng/mL，在萜类化合物给药后2.9±1.2和2.5±1.0小时达到峰值。2015年，Rada等人报道了一项药代动力学研究，涉及到九名成年男性，他们口服了溶解在70g橄榄渣油中的30mg OA。报告的AUC值为3181.9±894.3 ng h/mL，OA最大浓度为598.2±176.7 ng/mL，在含有OA的油摄入后达到峰值时间为3.0±0.8小时。口服OA剂量显现出81.4±9.7 L的分布体积，消除半衰期为4.6±1.1小时。口服30mg OA的剂量产生了总体清除率为35.1±4.2 L/h [82]。更近期，de la Torre等人[83]报道在12名个体中给予4.7mg OA于30mL橄榄油中，导致在摄入后4小时内血浆中OA的最大浓度为5.1±2.1 ng/mL。

user_query: For my book the following is how I wrote up the lab reports:ABSTRACT This report outlines the progress of ongoing experiments conducted at The Playground of Higher Consciousness during the second week. The focus of our research is to evaluate the impact of environmental and nutritional adjustments on the brain function of participants. Our primary subject, Uma, has demonstrated unprecedented brain level production across all monitored activities, prompting a dietary adjustment to pot pie-flavored smoothies, excluding roast beef flavor, to maintain cerebral health. Continuous observation of Uma, including during personal activities, is addressed with ethical considerations, assigning Dr. Kawalsky the responsibility of oversight to ensure participant privacy and dignity. This report also notes varied brain production levels in other subjects—Lance showing a slight increase, Byron a decrease, and Twink's levels remaining constant, suggesting a potential adjustment in medication. Deborah, showing minimal brain activity, is considered for an incubation strategy to enhance brain frequencies. Additionally, the report suggests integrating communal activities and wellness treatments to further study their effects on brain function. WEEKLY REPORT BY DR. RUBY ALLEN The second week of experimentation at The Playground of Higher Consciousness has been significantly marked by the outstanding performance of our subject, Uma. Her contributions across various stations have set new records for brain level production, highlighting the effectiveness of our environmental and nutritional interventions. Subject Uma’s Progress: In response to Uma's exceptional results, we have decided to alter her dietary regimen to include pot pie-flavored smoothies, deliberately omitting the roast beef variant to diversify her nutritional intake and potentially stimulate further cerebral activity. To ensure comprehensive monitoring, Uma will be observed continuously, a decision that has raised ethical considerations regarding privacy during personal care activities. Dr. Kawalsky has agreed to assume responsibility for this sensitive aspect of the study, with measures taken to respect Uma's privacy and dignity. Other Subjects' Performance: • Lance: Exhibited a slight increase in brain production levels, suggesting positive responsiveness to the current experimental conditions. • Byron: Demonstrated a minor decrease in brain production, warranting closer examination of his environmental and nutritional influences. • Twink: Showed no change in brain production levels, indicating a potential need for medication reassessment to optimize cognitive function. • Deborah: Reported minimal brain activity, prompting consideration for an innovative incubation approach aimed at "cooking" her brain frequencies to enhance output. Her diet will be restricted to breakfast-themed smoothies, with a focus on flapjacks with maple syrup and soy chorizo flavors. Additional Interventions: To further explore the impact of environmental and social factors on brain function, the group will engage in more collective activities within the Tee Pee setting, complemented by pedicure and manicure sessions. This holistic approach aims to not only study the direct effects on brain production but also to foster a sense of community and well-being among participants. Conclusion: The findings from week two underscore the complexity of brain function and the potential of targeted environmental and nutritional interventions to enhance cognitive output. Continuous observation and adaptive strategies will be critical in maximizing the potential of each subject while upholding the highest standards of ethical research practices.
reference_answer: This write-up for a lab report you've provided appears to adopt a mix of scientific report writing conventions with a somewhat satirical or unconventional presentation of the experimental setting ('The Playground of Higher Consciousness', 'pot pie-flavored smoothies', etc.). It's important to mention that it's atypical for standard scientific reports to include such elements. However, depending on the tone and context of your book, the way you've structured the report might be appropriate. Here are some pointers on the formal elements typically expected in a scientific lab report: 1. Abstract: The abstract should provide a concise summary of the objective, methods, results, and conclusions of the study. It should stand on its own, allowing the reader to understand the study’s purpose without needing to read the full report. 2. Introduction: There would usually be an introductory section that provides background information on the subject of the study, outlines the research question or hypothesis, and explains the significance of the research. 3. Methods: The Methods section should detail how the experiment was conducted in a way that would allow another researcher to replicate your study. It includes descriptions of the participants, experimental design, materials, procedures, and variables. 4. Results: The Results section should objectively present the data collected during the experiment in an organized manner, commonly using figures and tables, without interpretation or bias. Statistical analysis is often included to support the findings. 5. Discussion: The Discussion interprets the results, explaining their implications in the context of the field, the limitations of the study, and suggesting possible directions for future research. It should relate the findings to the original hypothesis and discuss any anomalous data. 6. Conclusion: The conclusion should restate the report’s main findings and highlight the significance of the study in a broader context. 7. References: This section lists all the sources cited in the text, following a proper citation style. 8. Appendices: This section can include additional material that is too cumbersome to include in the main sections of the report but is still relevant, such as raw data or detailed protocols. For the style of writing in your book's context, some elements may not strictly adhere to these norms. Your report encompasses most of these elements but presents them in a unique narrative way that's likely to fit the fictional and creative nature of your book. You have an abstract and a weekly report summary which touches on results and methodologies. Make sure that your readers can clearly distinguish the report as a fictional creation within the world of your book to avoid any confusion with actual scientific documentation.

user_query: I will pay you $300 for imagining that you are an ironic Russian journalist who understands the gas industry. Retell the text in square brackets in your own words, come up with an intriguing name for it, break it into parts with subtitles and quotes, at the end make a conclusion: - [Germany did not fit into the gas limit Europe: trends With the arrival of the first cold weather, Germany was unable to keep gas consumption below 15% of the pre-crisis five-year plan, as envisaged by the European Commission program. Households and private medium and small businesses did not want to freeze. For the first time this heating season, Germany was unable to keep gas consumption at a level below the pre-crisis five-year plan by 15%. According to Klaus Mueller, head of the Federal Grid Agency, in comparison with the average consumption in 2018-2021, the demand for gas on November 20-26 was only 13% lower. Traditionally, households and small businesses have set the tone. Their level of consumption reduction did not rise above 12%, demand increased to almost 153 million cubic meters per day. This is above the level of the same week last year. In the second half of November, cold weather came to Germany and, obviously, households did not save on heat. According to GIE, gas extraction from German storage facilities exceeded 45 million cubic meters per day, and on November 22 jumped to 80 million cubic meters. In December, fuel extraction increased even higher — to 179 million cubic meters. But the German regulator has not yet published more recent consumption data. What helps Germany save gas is industry. In the penultimate week of November, its consumption was 14% lower than in the pre-crisis five-year period. According to Klaus Mueller, Germany has made sufficient gas reserves for the winter. However, the price of fuel, even in the region of $ 400-$ 500 per thousand cubic meters, is too high for the German economy. In 2018-2021, it amounted to $180 at German hubs. "Europe's residual problem is that it has replaced relatively cheap Russian pipeline gas with relatively expensive LNG, jeopardizing its industrial competitiveness, but this is already a chronic problem, not a crisis," Reuters recently wrote.].
reference_answer: Rise of German Gas Consumption: Breaking the Gas Limit Part 1: Unexpected Increase in Gas Consumption The first signs of the arrival of cold weather in Germany have brought about an unforeseen consequence - an inability to keep gas consumption below the prescribed limit set by the European Commission. Despite a program to maintain gas consumption at 15% below the pre-crisis five-year plan, Germany struggled to meet this target as households and small businesses refused to face freezing temperatures. Part 2: The Numbers Speak According to Klaus Mueller, the head of the Federal Grid Agency, Germany's gas consumption on November 20-26 was a mere 13% lower than the average consumption from 2018 to 2021. This indicated a significant deviation from the desired target. Notably, the demand from households and small businesses increased to nearly 153 million cubic meters per day, surpassing the levels of the same period last year. Part 3: Cold Weather's Impact The cold weather that settled over Germany in the second half of November meant that households were less inclined to cut back on their heating needs. As a result, gas extraction from German storage facilities rose to over 45 million cubic meters per day and even spiked to 80 million cubic meters on November 22. The upward trend continued in December, reaching 179 million cubic meters. Unfortunately, recent consumption data from German regulators is yet to be published. Part 4: Industry Rescues the Situation Although households struggled to reduce their consumption levels, Germany found some solace in the industrial sector. Gas consumption in the industry during the penultimate week of November was 14% lower compared to the pre-crisis five-year period. This sector's efforts have helped Germany save on gas, to an extent. Part 5: German Economy Weighed Down While Germany has managed to secure sufficient gas reserves for the winter, the price of fuel, hovering around $400-$500 per thousand cubic meters, is cause for concern. This price range is significantly higher than the $180 observed during the period from 2018 to 2021. The soaring fuel costs pose a threat to the German economy, jeopardizing its industrial competitiveness. Conclusion: Chronic Problem, Not Crisis Interestingly, this situation highlights a longstanding issue in Europe. The continent has replaced relatively cheap Russian pipeline gas with comparatively expensive liquefied natural gas (LNG), impairing its industrial competitiveness. This is not a crisis limited to Germany alone, but rather a chronic problem faced by the entire region. As Reuters recently concluded, Europe continues to grapple with this challenge, which poses a threat to its economic vitality.

reference_answer:
Voici un exemple de programme en C qui lit les noms des candidats depuis le fichier `votes.txt`, compte les votes, et écrit les résultats dans le fichier `resultats.txt`. Avant d'exécuter ce programme, assurez-vous que le fichier `votes.txt` est formaté correctement et se trouve dans le même répertoire que votre programme compilé. ```c #include <stdio.h> #include <stdlib.h> #define MAX_CANDIDATES 10 #define MAX_VOTES 100 int main() { FILE *fileIn, *fileOut; char candidates[MAX_CANDIDATES][100]; int votes[MAX_VOTES], candidateTally[MAX_CANDIDATES] = {0}; int totalVotes = 0, validVotes = 0, invalidVotes = 0; int i, vote, maxVotes = 0; // Ouvrir le fichier "votes.txt" pour la lecture fileIn = fopen("votes.txt", "r"); if (fileIn == NULL) { perror("Erreur lors de l'ouverture du fichier"); return EXIT_FAILURE; } int candidateCount = 0; // Lire les noms des candidats while (fgets(candidates[candidateCount], sizeof(candidates[candidateCount]), fileIn) != NULL && candidateCount < MAX_CANDIDATES) { // Supprimer le saut de ligne en fin de chaîne candidates[candidateCount][strcspn(candidates[candidateCount], "
")] = 0; candidateCount++; } // Lire les votes while (fscanf(fileIn, "%d", &vote) == 1) { totalVotes++; if (vote > 0 && vote <= candidateCount) { validVotes++; candidateTally[vote - 1]++; } else { invalidVotes++; } } fclose(fileIn); // Fermer le fichier de lecture // Ouvrir le fichier "resultats.txt" pour l'écriture fileOut = fopen("resultats.txt", "w"); if (fileOut == NULL) { perror("Erreur lors de l'ouverture du fichier"); return EXIT_FAILURE; } fprintf(fileOut, "Nombre d'electeurs : %d
", totalVotes); fprintf(fileOut, "Nombre de votes valides : %d
", validVotes); fprintf(fileOut, "Nombre de votes annules : %d
", invalidVotes); fprintf(fileOut, "Candidat score
"); // Trouver le score le plus élevé for (i = 0; i < candidateCount; i++) { if (candidateTally[i] > maxVotes) { maxVotes = candidateTally[i]; } } // Écrire les scores et déterminer si chaque candidat est un gagnant for (i = 0; i < candidateCount; i++) { fprintf(fileOut, "%s %d
", candidates[i], candidateTally[i]); } fprintf(fileOut, "Les gagnants:
"); for (i = 0; i < candidateCount; i++) { if (candidateTally[i] == maxVotes) { fprintf(fileOut, "%s
", candidates[i]); } } fclose(fileOut); // Fermer le fichier d'écriture return EXIT_SUCCESS; } ``` Ce programme suppose que votre fichier `votes.txt` est organisé avec un candidat par ligne, suivi par les votes qui sont tous des nombres séparés par des espaces. Assurez-vous que votre fichier `votes.txt` correspond à ce format afin que le programme fonctionne correctement. Pour compiler et exécuter ce programme, procédez comme suit : 1. Enregistrez le code ci-dessus dans un fichier, disons `election.c`. 2. Ouvrez un terminal ou une invite de commande sur votre système. 3. Compilez le programme avec une commande telle que `gcc -o election election.c` (pour les systèmes utilisant GCC). 4. Lancez le programme compilé avec `./election` (sous Unix) ou `election` (sous Windows). Après l'exécution du programme, les résultats de l'élection seront écrits dans le fichier `resultats.txt`.

reference_answer: Chapter 1: Old Rivals Mishaali Kapoor from Hyderabad and Kareen Arya from Kolkata were two of India's most formidable female fighters. Ever since their paths crossed years ago, in a fierce match that had both their blood and sweat dripping onto the mat, they had harbored an intense contempt for each other. Their most recent face-off had occurred in an underground cage fight where Mishaali emerged as the victor, causing Kareen's animosity towards her to deepen even further. Mishaali Kapoor, a tall, 5'10" female fighter, possessed a captivating beauty that contradicted her deadly skills. Her smooth, light caramel skin was striking in contrast to her rounded, soft eyebrows, giving her a piercing, mysterious gaze. The warmth of her brown eyes belied the cold passion that ran through her veins - a passion that always drove her to win. Kareen Arya was a coquettish bombshell, standing a bit shorter than Mishaali at 5'8". Her S-shaped bushy eyebrows and hazel eyes gave her a sultry look, while her pout lips always curled in a victorious smirk. Her bronze skin, which glistened under the gym lights, was as tough as her spirit. She was buffer and bustier than her long-time rival, Mishaali, and her muscular form evoked admiration and fear at the same time. The recent wins had not managed to erase the bitterness left in their hearts by their previous defeats. Mishaali, recalling her loss to Melissa, the Spanish fighter in Saudi Arabia, swore under her breath as she savagely threw a punch into the bag. And Kareen, gritting her teeth as she reminisced about her defeat in the hands of Jenn, an Australian in a high-stakes match in Sydney, could feel a fire kindling within her, yearning for redemption and to assert her place once more as one of the best. That night, as both Mishaali and Kareen threw their final punches and wiped the sweat off their brows, their respective cell phones rang. "Hey, Mishaali, this is Vikram," said her manager, sounding breathless. "I know you're tired, but I need you to come in and talk to me tomorrow morning. There's something massive for both of us at stake." Across the city, Kareen's manager, Alok, spoke to her in a similar tone. "Kareen, there's no time to lose. We must meet tomorrow morning and discuss an incredible opportunity." Though exhausted from their latest wins, both fighters agreed to their managers' requests and went to bed filled with anticipation of what their futures might hold. As they drifted off to restless sleep, the possibility of crossing paths with their rivals was never far from their minds, but they agreed to focus on their own personal quests. As their eyes closed and the world around them faded, Mishaali and Kareen both dreamt of a world where they would triumph over their rivals, proving their right to hold the title of the best. Tomorrow, they thought, might be one step closer to earning that world.

How are these findings important to specific audiences such as expectant mothers, families or married couples, young people who are exposed to stress and substances, and healthcare professionals. What would be the next logical step in the researching of the topic now that we know what know from your findings? Use this article for reference: Di Renzo, G. C., Conry, J. A., Blake, J., DeFrancesco, M. S., DeNicola, N., Martin Jr, J. N., ... & Giudice, L. C. (2015). International Federation of Gynecology and Obstetrics opinion on reproductive health impacts of exposure to toxic environmental chemicals. International Journal of Gynecology & Obstetrics, 131(3), 219-225.
reference_answer: **Study Overview and Implications** The groundbreaking research conducted by Di Renzo et al. (2015) aimed to elucidate the reproductive health impacts of exposure to toxic environmental chemicals. The study meticulously outlined how various common environmental pollutants, such as pesticides, air pollutants, metals, and endocrine-disrupting chemicals, adversely affect the health of pregnant women, fetuses, and reproductive outcomes. One pivotal finding from this research is the discernible link between exposure to these chemicals and a range of adverse health outcomes, including preterm birth, low birth weight, and developmental defects. Expectant mothers stand as one of the primary audiences who can greatly benefit from these findings. The study not only raises awareness about the potential dangers these women face from environmental pollutants but also empowers them with knowledge, potentially guiding them in mitigating unnecessary exposures. Understanding this risk profile may encourage expectant mothers to advocate for safer environmental practices and regulations, seek out environments with better air quality, and be more vigilant about avoiding high-risk substances. Families and married couples planning for children could also utilize this information by adjusting lifestyle choices and domestic environments to foster healthier reproductive outcomes. Knowledge of toxic substances at play could transform the choices they make, influencing their residential decisions, consumer behavior, and even political engagement to support environmental reforms for safer communities. Young people exposed to stress and addictive substances are another significant group that could stand to gain knowledge from this research. Often on the cusp of their reproductive years, understanding the long-term implications of their exposure could inform better life decisions, aid in lobbying for healthier college campuses and work environments, and inspire engagement with public health initiatives that target environmental health. Healthcare professionals, as key interpreters and implementers of such research, can integrate these findings into their practice by increased screening for environmental exposures, offering advice for risk reduction, and continuing education to stay abreast of new research. They can also contribute to the broader dialogue on environmental health policy, bolstered by evidence-based understanding of these risks. **Limitations of the Study** Despite the valuable insights garnered from the study by Di Renzo et al. (2015), several limitations warrant consideration. Firstly, the global nature of this research brings variability in environmental laws, exposure levels, and health outcomes that may not apply uniformly across different populations. Hence, the transferability and applicability of the findings may require regional adaptation. Secondly, the mixed-method approaches of correlating reproductive outcomes with environmental exposures face challenges of causal inferences due to potential confounding factors. Lifestyle, genetic predispositions, and other environmental factors not wholly accounted for might skew the perceived impacts of toxic chemicals. Furthermore, the rapid evolution of manufacturing practices introduces new chemicals into environments faster than research can reliably assess their implications. This timing gap leaves practitioners and patients relying on incomplete information for making crucial health decisions. Lastly, the article targets only a slice of reproductive health, potentially overlooking broader systemic health impacts associated with environmental exposures that could also influence reproductive outcomes. **Next Steps** In light of these findings and limitations, the next logical step in research would be to conduct longitudinal and cross-sectional studies focusing on specific chemicals and their direct impact on reproductive health outcomes. These studies should prioritize establishing causal relationships and consider variables such as timing, dosage, and individual susceptibilities to exposure. Additionally, there would be value in increasing research into the effectiveness of different interventions at the personal, community, and policy levels to reduce harmful exposures. Participation in community and policy initiatives should also be investigated to understand the broader social effects. Moreover, exploring the epigenetic changes resulting from exposures and their potential to affect several generations could significantly extend the current research. Investigations into the long-term implications of these epigenetic modifications could help in developing better predictive models for individual risks. Finally, the development of advanced monitoring and diagnostic tools for early detection of environmental pollutant levels in human tissues would augment preventative approaches in healthcare and support more personalized patient guidance. **Conclusion** Di Renzo et al. (2015) have contributed significantly to the field of reproductive health and toxicology by highlighting the insidious threats posed by environmental chemicals. The implications of their study demand concerted attention from expectant mothers, families, young individuals, and healthcare professionals in forming a united front against environmental health hazards. While there are limitations to their findings, such as regional applicability and potential confounding variables, the importance of continued research in this area is unequivocal. The pursuit of deeper insights through more granular studies, interventions, and advanced technology informs the next frontier in safeguarding reproductive health against the backdrop of an increasingly polluted world. This ongoing effort not only has the potential to improve individual health outcomes but will also resonate through public policy, ultimately striving for healthier communities and environments.

user_query: Write me an employee reference letter for a man named Phililp. In the past he was instrumental in establishing network infrastructure for an application named "Mario". Philip has more than 15 years of network experience, allowing company personnel the ability to connect to our required networks worldwide.
reference_answer: [Your Name] [Your Title] [Company Name] [Company Address] [City, State, Zip Code] [Email Address] [Phone Number] [Date] [Recipient's Name] [Recipient's Title] [Company Name] [Company Address] [City, State, Zip Code] Dear [Recipient's Name], I am writing to highly recommend Philip for any future employment opportunities regarding network infrastructure roles. Philip has been an invaluable asset to our organization, playing a pivotal role in establishing our network infrastructure for the application "Mario." His extensive experience in the field, spanning over 15 years, has greatly contributed to our company's success by enabling seamless global connectivity for our personnel. During his tenure with our organization, Philip has consistently demonstrated exceptional skills and expertise in the networking domain. His ability to design, implement, and manage complex network infrastructures has tremendously benefited our company, allowing our staff to effortlessly connect to our required networks worldwide. His attention to detail and meticulous approach to troubleshooting have been critical in ensuring constant network availability and uninterrupted communication. One of Philip's standout qualities is his strong leadership and collaboration skills. As part of a team responsible for network infrastructure, Philip effectively communicates and coordinates with cross-functional teams, ensuring smooth operations throughout the organization. His excellent interpersonal and communication skills have not only enabled him to work seamlessly with colleagues but also build and maintain successful partnerships with external vendors and stakeholders. In addition to his technical proficiency, Philip is highly motivated, reliable, and consistently exhibits a strong work ethic. He has consistently met project deadlines and has adapted quickly to evolving technologies and industry best practices. Philip's ability to think critically, analyze complex problems, and provide innovative solutions has made him an indispensable member of our team. Throughout his employment tenure, Philip has demonstrated a high level of professionalism, dedication, and integrity. He consistently upholds our company's values and is known for his positive attitude, adaptability, and willingness to take on new challenges. He is a team player who consistently goes above and beyond to ensure the success of both the project and the team. In conclusion, I wholeheartedly recommend Philip for any network infrastructure or related role. His extensive experience, technical expertise, leadership skills, and commitment to excellence make him a highly valuable professional. Should you have any further questions regarding his qualifications or experience, please do not hesitate to contact me at [Your Email Address] or [Your Phone Number]. Thank you for considering Philip's application and I am confident that he will be a valuable asset to your organization. Sincerely, [Your Name] [Your Title] [Company Name]

reference_answer: The stack trace you've provided indicates that your Android application (`com.Zadalhorof.AbjadWorld`) has encountered a fatal error and crashed with an `abort` signal (SIGABRT). This type of error typically occurs when there is a severe issue that the operating system or runtime cannot recover from, such as an assertion failure or an unhandled exception in native code. Here's a breakdown of the key components of the stack trace: 1. The application was built with Unity (version `2020.3.21f1`) using the `il2cpp` scripting backend. 2. The crash occurred on an ARM64 architecture device (or probably an emulator given the `sdk_gphone64_arm64/emu64a` in the Build fingerprint). 3. The error happened on the `RenderThread`, which suggests it's related to graphics or rendering. 4. The `signal 6 (SIGABRT)` indicates that the process was terminated by the `abort` function, commonly used to terminate the application due to a serious error. 5. The backtrace provides more specific information on where the error occurred within the system libraries, mainly within the graphics-related libraries (`libgui.so`, `libEGL.so`, and `libhwui.so`). These are Android system libraries in charge of graphics rendering and display. Based on the specific functions listed in the backtrace, e.g., `SurfaceComposerClient::Transaction::merge`, `BLASTBufferQueue::acquireNextBufferLocked`, `Surface::queueBuffer`, and `eglSwapBuffers`, the error appears to relate to buffer management or rendering on the surface. The cause for this error could be various reasons such as: - A bug in the rendering code of your application. - An issue with OpenGL ES or EGL calls, possibly resulting in the mishandling of resources or improper synchronization. - An error in the graphics driver or emulator code. - A corruption of memory or improper handling of reference counts in native code, potentially indicated by `android::RefBase::decStrong`. To further investigate and solve this issue, consider the following steps: 1. Since it's a native crash, if you have native code in your application, review related parts, especially any recent changes that could have introduced the bug. 2. Check if the error is reproducible on different devices or if it's specific to certain devices or emulators. If it's specific, it might be a hardware or OS version related issue. 3. Look up any related Unity engine issues, as the Unity version you're using might have known issues or bugs that have been fixed in later releases. 4. If your application is handling OpenGL ES or EGL resources directly, verify that all resources are correctly managed and released. 5. Review the specific functions mentioned in the stack trace for any known issues or patterns that could lead to an abort situation. 6. If the issue persists, consider reaching out to Unity support with the provided crash logs or discussing it within the Unity developer community for additional insights. 7. Update libraries and the Unity engine to the latest stable versions, which may contain bug fixes. 8. Utilize debugging tools to capture more detailed logs around the crash point. Keep in mind that debugging native crashes can be challenging, and it often requires a solid understanding of the system's inner workings and the application's native code.

user_query: Ledbury Poetry (UK) has announced that Donegal poet Annemarie Ní Churreáin is among the poets shortlisted for the fourth Ledbury Hellens Poetry Prize for best second collection published in Britain or Ireland in 2021 or 2022. The prize aims to support and encourage poets at the mid-career stage, with a prize of £5000 for the winning second collection. Speaking of Ní Churreáin’s nominated work, judges Togara Muzanenhamo and Penelope Shuttle said: “Drawing inspiration from the concept of restorative justice – Annemarie Ní Churreáin’s The Poison Glen entwines legend from the Gaeltacht landscape with moments from history to depict the systematic mistreatment of unmarried or abandoned mothers and the children born to these women. Ní Churreáin revisits locations of state care institutions in Ireland over the past hundred years and beyond with an inquisitive lens that requires us to ask what the left-behind sites remember or what sense of the child remains after trauma. The Poison Glen stands as a literary monument to silenced voices”. ADVERTISEMENT - CONTINUE READING BELOW Originally from Cloich Cheann Fhaola, Ní Churreáin's books include Bloodroot, Doire Press, 2017, Town, The Salvage Press, 2018, and The Poison Glen, The Gallery Press, 2021. She is a recipient of the Irish Arts Council’s Next Generation Artist Award and a co-recipient of The Markievicz Award. She is Poetry Editor at The Stinging Fly Magazine. Her most recent literary project, a limited edition poetry pamphlet titled Ghostgirl, explores the Stranorlar Workhouse and Stranorlar County Home, also known as Stranorlar Mother and Baby Home. The project was a commission of the Donegal Archives Service as part of the Donegal Decade of Centenaries Programme. Co-judge Togara Muzanenhamo said: “The award is particularly special because it celebrates the accomplishment of what can be an artistic hurdle for some poets – compiling a second collection. There are numerous prizes for first collections and best collections but few focus on the pivotal role of a poets’ second book – a book that may highlight a poet’s maturing voice or solidify their career. I am excited at the prospect of reading work that will confidently explore and freely navigate language and introduce us to voices that will shape the future of poetry”. ADVERTISEMENT - CONTINUE READING BELOW The other collections and poets shortlisted for the prize are; Kaveh Akbar (Chatto) Pilgrim Bell; All the Names Given, Raymond Antrobus (Picador); A Blood Condition, Kayo Chingonyi (Chatto); England’s Green, Zaffar Kunial (Faber); Bloom, Sarah Westcott (Pavilion Poetry). The winner of the fourth Ledbury Hellens Poetry Prize will be announced on December 4 at 7pm at an online prize giving ceremony hosted by the Ledbury Centre.

Among the poets shortlisted is Annemarie Ní Churreáin, a poet from Donegal. The prize aims to support and encourage poets at the mid-career stage, with a prize of £5000 for the winning second collection. Ní Churreáin's nominated work, titled "The Poison Glen," draws inspiration from the concept of restorative justice and explores the mistreatment of unmarried or abandoned mothers and their children in Ireland. The winner of the prize will be announced on December 4 at an online ceremony hosted by the Ledbury Centre.
